codeReview.summary: The change reintroduced epsilon-based tolerance for idempotent cursor advancement, but the selected tolerance (1e-9 seconds) was not robust enough for GRDB/SQLite timestamp round-trips, so the fix did not reliably satisfy the stated full-suite stabilization goal.

issues.description: Using `monotonicEpsilon = 1e-9` in the monotonic guard is too strict for persisted Date precision/serialization behavior; this still allows idempotent equality checks to fail in practice. Evidence: subsequent commit `ae68a630f8999a62eec523b99a49bec6203a1613` in the same area explicitly raises epsilon to 1ms because nanosecond tolerance was insufficient.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but transcript evidence does not show reading all files required by `indexing-efficiency-worker` step 1 (notably `.factory/library/user-testing.md`).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file requires reading `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; transcript skeleton shows reads for mission/validation/backfill files and architecture, but no read of `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Timestamp precision expectations for cursor monotonicity checks are not captured in shared guidance, which likely contributed to a too-small epsilon choice and immediate follow-up correction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit `33f9ac23ddb36df2d2bc9eff18c73a67f2e5d5c4` introduced 1ns epsilon; later commit `ae68a630f8999a62eec523b99a49bec6203a1613` revised it to 1ms due to GRDB/SQLite datetime round-trip precision.

summary: Reviewed fix commit `33f9ac23ddb36df2d2bc9eff18c73a67f2e5d5c4` against the prior fast-surfaces scrutiny failure. The patch direction was correct, but tolerance selection was insufficient, so this feature is marked fail for not reliably meeting the stabilization requirement.
